notes: WIN-FND-003. Ledgers the elevation triad from docs/design/01-foundations.md sections 2.1 and 4. The Border/Frame channel is the only elevation dimension the native definition implements today; tonal surfaces are real palette roles; shadows and the scrim are prototype-only. The shadow rows are pinned to the doc verbatim and cross-checked against the prototype's box-shadow literals so the doc stays the single source of truth. Native opacity is not even parseable in the widget-definition schema. Source evidence only: runtime_verified is false. Native shadow/scrim/opacity rendering, pixel/MATRIX capture, and the unassigned window-shell elevation owner remain the separate build/runtime gates.

border.note: The outlined Material frame (ControlType::Frame / ControlPart::Border) is the only compiled elevation channel: a stroke-thin @outline-variant outline over an @surface-container fill at @corner-container radius, inset 2px so grouped children sit inside the border.

scrim.note: The modal scrim is a prototype presentation value with no native role (01-foundations.md 2.1). The three rgba literals must be byte-identical across the doc and the prototype palette.

shadows.note: Every section-4 shadow-table row is prototype-only; the native definition adds no shadow drawable. The doc is authoritative and the prototype's box-shadow literals are reconciled to match it. doc_row_count guards against a native shadow row being slipped into the table un-ledgered.

opacity.note: Opacity is not implemented and not even latent: no drawable element in definition.xml carries an opacity/shadow/blur/elevation attribute, and the widget-definition reader never parses one. The reader's shadowColor binds the legacy StyleSettings 3D-bevel slot (a distinct concept covered by check-material-theme.py), not this row's MD3 elevation shadow.
